notes.note: The amended lex Clodia (rogatio de pernicie mea), which now interdicted Cicero within a fixed radius of Italy. Its supplementary clause is the 'amendment' whose harsh terms he reports next.

The amended Clodian law barred Cicero from coming within (variously reported) four hundred miles of Italy; here he reads the limit as five hundred. The clause is why he abandons the nearer refuge of Malta and pushes on toward Greece.

notes.anchor: being at Malta was not allowed
notes.note: Malta (Melita) lay too close to Italy to satisfy the law's distance clause, so Cicero could not take refuge there. He redirects toward Brundisium and the longer crossing east.
'Pomponius' is Atticus himself --- T. Pomponius Atticus, the recipient of every letter in this book --- addressed by his nomen as an intimacy, not a third party.
